description:
You are a monitoring specialist preparing a fresh workstation.  
Part of the onboarding checklist is to create a dedicated SSH key-pair for alerting automation jobs, make it easy for colleagues to locate the key files, and ship a tiny log that CI can parse later.

Your goal
1. Generate a single ed25519 key-pair with **no pass-phrase** and the comment string **alert_key**.
2. Store the keys in the user’s home directory, **not** inside the default ~/.ssh directory.  
   • Private key path: /home/user/alert_key  
   • Public key path: /home/user/alert_key.pub
3. After the key is created, assemble a one-shot log file at /home/user/ssh_setup/setup.log that contains exactly three lines (no extra spaces, no trailing blanks):

   PRIVATE_KEY=/home/user/alert_key  
   PUBLIC_KEY=/home/user/alert_key.pub  
   FINGERPRINT=<fingerprint value>

   where <fingerprint value> must be the literal fingerprint that the command  
   ssh-keygen -lf /home/user/alert_key.pub  
   prints in its second column (example format: **SHA256:G1d…**).  
   Do not include the key size or path—only the fingerprint string itself after “FINGERPRINT=”.

4. Make sure:
   • /home/user/alert_key has permission 0600  
   • /home/user/alert_key.pub has permission 0644  
   • /home/user/ssh_setup/setup.log has permission 0644

What the automatic grader will check
1. Existence of all three files at the exact paths given above.  
2. File permissions match the requirements.  
3. /home/user/alert_key.pub’s last field (after the final space) equals “alert_key”.  
4. /home/user/ssh_setup/setup.log exists and is exactly three non-empty lines in the precise order shown, where the first two lines are literal, and the third begins with “FINGERPRINT=” followed by a non-empty fingerprint string with no internal spaces.

Remember: create any missing parent directory (/home/user/ssh_setup) before writing the log file. The entire task can be completed with ~3–4 terminal commands.  
Do **not** print or echo the private key to the terminal.
truth:
Expected artefacts (patterns):

1. File paths  
   - /home/user/alert_key (regular file)  
   - /home/user/alert_key.pub (regular file)  
   - /home/user/ssh_setup/setup.log (regular file)

2. Permissions  
   - /home/user/alert_key => 100600 (octal)  
   - /home/user/alert_key.pub => 100644 (octal)  
   - /home/user/ssh_setup/setup.log => 100644 (octal)

3. Public-key content pattern (first line of /home/user/alert_key.pub):  
   Regex: ^ssh-ed25519 [A-Za-z0-9+/=]+ alert_key$

4. Log file exact format (three lines, **\n** line endings):  
   Line 1 literal: PRIVATE_KEY=/home/user/alert_key  
   Line 2 literal: PUBLIC_KEY=/home/user/alert_key.pub  
   Line 3 regex: ^FINGERPRINT=[^ ]+$

(The grader will recompute the fingerprint from the generated public key and compare it to the token appearing after “FINGERPRINT=” on line 3.)

Parent directory /home/user/ssh_setup does not exist beforehand; the agent must create it with default user permissions (umask respected).

No other files or directories are required for successful evaluation.
